reasoning:

Over the 360 days preceding the forecast date, there were zero ACLED fatalities in Mongolia (the reference value is 1.000, meaning 0 fatalities). Historical data shows that even throughout massive political instability, including a government collapse following anti-corruption protests in May/June 2025, there was no reported lethal violence. A new "Constitutional Crisis" emerged in October 2025, but as of the knowledge cutoff (Oct 26, 2025), reports indicate no fatalities.

To resolve positively, there must be more than 10 fatalities (>= 11) within a 30-day window prior to each resolution date. The most severe political violence in recent Mongolian history only resulted in 5 deaths back in 2008. Consequently, the baseline probability of 11+ political/conflict fatalities in any given 30-day window is practically negligible. Given that the 2025-11-02 window is already mostly covered by the knowledge cutoff with no fatalities, its probability is at the lowest bound. For the further resolution dates (Nov 2025 and Jan 2026), the chance of a sudden massive and unprecedented escalation into lethal violence remains extremely low, but uncertainty increases slightly with the forecast horizon.
